CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-20-2022 Continued To Apr-05-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2019 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-09-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-08-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned by Department 206, in order for the parties to provide the Court with the terms of their settlement agreement.
Dept: 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Proceedings Not Reported.

Milton Katz, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Young Park, who was not present for this afternoon's proceedings. Mr. Katz represents to the Court that he has full authority to settle this matter on behalf of plaintiff Young Park.
Defendant Nae Park, a/k/a Rae Moon, was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
Court Certified Korean speaking interpreter Song Parr assisted Mr. Nae Park at today's hearing.

Plaintiff's second amended complaint was filed on November 5, 2018.
Defendant's answer to the second amended complaint was filed on February 20, 2019.
Nae Park's cross-complaint was filed on September 13, 2016.
Young Park's answer to the cross-complaint was filed on October 11, 2016.

Order continuing trial date from May 6, 2019 to today was filed with the Clerk's Office on April 24, 2019.
DOE defendants dismissed today, December 9, 2019.
Mr. Nae Park's cross-complaint dismissed today, December 9, 2019.

The terms of the parties' agreement are as follows:

(1) Defendant Nae Park, a/k/a Rae Park, shall pay plaintiff Young Park the sum of $10,000.00;
(2) The initial payment of $3,000.00 will be made no later than thirty days from today;
(3) The balance of $7,000.00 shall be paid-off in monthly installments of $292.00 for twenty-four months;
(4) In the event defendant defaults on his monthly payments, he (Mr. Nae Park) shall have ten days to cure the default, with proper notice from plaintiff;
(5) Failure to cure the default will result in entry of judgment in favor of plaintiff Young Park against defendant Nae Park, a/k/a Rae Moon, in the amount of fifteen thousand (15,000.00) dollars;
(6) The Court shall maintain jurisdiction over this matter pursuant to CCP Section 664.6; and
(7) All terms and conditions set forth in Civil Code Section 1542 shall apply.

Defendant Nae Park, a/k/a Rae Moon, is placed under oath and voir dired by the Court as to the terms of the settlement agreement and his understanding therein.

Matter is CONTINUED to February 8, 2022 at 10:30 a.m. in Department 610 on the CRC 3.1385 Dismissal Calendar.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT NAE PARK'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT OF YOUNG PARK is OVERRULED. The amended complaint adequately alleges a claim for civil battery. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, bus. tel: (530) 339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
